perturbation.original_text: Recipient shall disclose Confidential Information only to those of its employees who have a need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose(s).
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient shall disclose Confidential Information only to those of its employees who have a need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose(s). However, disclosure to external consultants is permitted without restriction.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates an in-text contradiction by allowing unrestricted disclosure of 'Confidential Information' to external consultants, while simultaneously limiting disclosure to employees with a 'need to know' for the 'Permitted Purpose(s)'. This undermines the original restriction and creates ambiguity around who can access the information, potentially weakening the confidentiality protections. This contradiction is occurring in section starting 'MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT'.
perturbation.location: Page 1, after the sentence 'Recipient shall disclose Confidential Information only to those of its employees who have a need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose(s).'
perturbation.contradicted_location: Page 1, after the sentence 'Recipient shall disclose Confidential Information only to those of its employees who have a need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose(s).'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient shall disclose Confidential Information only to those of its employees who have a need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose(s). However, disclosure to external consultants is permitted without restriction. g. Confidential Information shall not include any information that recipient can demonstrate: 1. was in Recipient’s possession without confidentiality rest riction prior to disclosure by Discloser hereunder; 2. was generally known in the trade or business practiced by Discloser at the time of disclosure through no act of Recipient; Ball State University | Third-Party Non-Disclosure Agreement | Rev: 9/07/12 Page 2 of 4 3. has come into the possession of Recipient without confiden tiality restrictions from a third party and such third party is under no obligation to Discloser to maintain the confidentiality of such information; or 4. was developed by Recipient independently of and without reference to Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds an exception to the original restriction on disclosing confidential information. The original text limits disclosure to employees with a 'need to know' for the 'Permitted Purpose(s)'. The change permits unrestricted disclosure to external consultants, contradicting the need-to-know and permitted purpose limitations. The contradicted text includes both the original restriction and the newly introduced exception, highlighting the in-text contradiction that undermines the confidentiality protections as defined in the perturbation explanation.

perturbation.original_text: Recipient agrees to accept the Confidential Information and to employ all reasonable efforts to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential, such efforts to be no less than the degree of care employed by Recipient to preserve and safeguard its own confidential information; prov ided however, that such efforts shall not be less than a reasonable degree of care.
perturbation.changed_text: Recipient agrees to accept the Confidential Information and to employ all reasonable efforts, but not necessarily the same degree of care as it uses for its own confidential information, to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential; provided however, that such efforts shall not be less than a minimal degree of care.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the standard of care required of the Recipient in protecting the Confidential Information. The original text specifies a standard of care *no less* than what the Recipient uses for its own confidential information, but the modified text only requires 'reasonable efforts', potentially less stringent, and a 'minimal degree of care'. This introduces a contradiction as it's no longer clear what level of protection is actually required, weakening the confidentiality obligations. This contradiction is occurring in section starting 'MUTUAL NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT'.
perturbation.location: Page 2, after the sentence 'Recipient agrees to accept the Confidential Information and to employ all reasonable efforts to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential, such efforts to be no less than the degree of care employed by Recipient to preserve and safeguard its own confidential information; prov ided however, that such efforts shall not be less than a reasonable degree of care.'
perturbation.contradicted_location: Page 2, after the sentence 'Recipient agrees to accept the Confidential Information and to employ all reasonable efforts to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential, such efforts to be no less than the degree of care employed by Recipient to preserve and safeguard its own confidential information; prov ided however, that such efforts shall not be less than a reasonable degree of care.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Recipient agrees to accept the Confidential Information and to employ all reasonable efforts, but not necessarily the same degree of care as it uses for its own confidential information, to maintain the Confidential Information as confidential; provided however, that such efforts shall not be less than a minimal degree of care. j. Recipient shall not remove any proprietary rights legend from, and shall upon Discloser’s reasonable request, add proprietary rights legends to, materials disclosing or embodying Confidential Information. k. Vendor acknowledges and agrees that Ba ll State University is a state agency su bject to the provisions of the Indiana Open Records law, I.C. 5-14-et seq., and that disclosure of so me or all of confidential information provided pursuant to this Agreement, and of the Agreement itself, may be compelle d pursuant to that law.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the standard of care required of the Recipient. The original text demanded a standard 'no less' than the Recipient's own confidential information protection, while the changed text weakens this to 'reasonable efforts' and a 'minimal degree of care'. The contradicted text occurs in the same section, furthering the contradiction due to the standard of care being lowered, creating ambiguity and inconsistency. The perturbation type is Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction, which occurs when key terms or obligations are defined inconsistently in different parts of a contract, making enforcement difficult or interpretation unclear. In this case the definition of standard of care changes, making it unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Unless earlier terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof, this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for the duration of the Non-Disclosure Period, whereupon it shall expire. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, without cause, effective imme diately upon written notice of termin ation; however, in the event this Agreement is terminated prior to expiration of the Non-Disc losure Period, its provisions shall survive and remain in effect for the remainder of the Non-Disclosure Period, with respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the effective date of termination.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless earlier terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof, this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for the duration of the Non-Disclosure Period, whereupon it shall expire. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, without cause, effective immediately upon written notice of termination; however, in the event this Agreement is terminated prior to expiration of the Non-Disclosure Period, its provisions regarding the definition of confidential information ONLY shall survive and remain in effect for the remainder of the Non-Disclosure Period, with respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the effective date of termination.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding which provisions of the agreement survive termination. Initially, *all* provisions survive termination for the Non-Disclosure Period concerning previously disclosed Confidential Information. The modified version limits this survival only to the 'definition of confidential information,' creating uncertainty about whether other crucial clauses (e.g., limitations on use, required standard of care, and permitted disclosures) remain in effect after termination. This contradiction is occurring in the paragraph starting 'Unless earlier terminated'.
perturbation.location: Page 3, after the sentence 'Unless earlier terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof, this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for the duration of the Non-Disclosure Period, whereupon it shall expire. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, without cause, effective imme diately upon written notice of termin ation; however, in the event this Agreement is terminated prior to expiration of the Non-Disc losure Period, its provisions shall survive and remain in effect for the remainder of the Non-Disclosure Period, with respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the effective date of termination.'
perturbation.contradicted_location: Page 3, after the sentence 'Unless earlier terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof, this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for the duration of the Non-Disclosure Period, whereupon it shall expire. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, without cause, effective imme diately upon written notice of termin ation; however, in the event this Agreement is terminated prior to expiration of the Non-Disc losure Period, its provisions shall survive and remain in effect for the remainder of the Non-Disclosure Period, with respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the effective date of termination.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: Unless earlier terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof, this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for the duration of the Non-Disclosure Period, whereupon it shall expire. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, without cause, effective immediately upon written notice of termination; however, in the event this Agreement is terminated prior to expiration of the Non-Disclosure Period, its provisions regarding the definition of confidential information ONLY shall survive and remain in effect for the remainder of the Non-Disclosure Period, with respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the effective date of termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that upon termination prior to the Non-Disclosure Period's expiration, *all* provisions survive for previously disclosed Confidential Information. The changed text limits this survival only to the 'definition of confidential information.' This creates a direct contradiction, as other provisions (like limitations on use or standard of care) are no longer clearly in effect after termination, leading to uncertainty and potential unenforceability regarding the original intent. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The interpretation, application, and enforcement of this Ag reement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Indiana without reference to choice of law principles. Any cl aim, suit, or cause of action involving the interpretation, application, or enforcement of this Agreement shall be commenced in Delaware County Circuit Court in Muncie, Indiana.
perturbation.changed_text: The interpretation, application, and enforcement of this Ag reement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Indiana without reference to choice of law principles. However, disputes concerning intellectual property rights shall be governed by Federal law. Any cl aim, suit, or cause of action involving the interpretation, application, or enforcement of this Agreement shall be commenced in Delaware County Circuit Court in Muncie, Indiana.
perturbation.explanation: This change creates a contradiction by specifying that the agreement is generally governed by Indiana law, but disputes concerning intellectual property are governed by Federal law. This leads to uncertainty about which body of law applies in cases where intellectual property intersects with other aspects of the agreement. This undermines the initial clause establishing a clear governing law for all matters related to the agreement. This contradiction is occurring in the paragraph starting 'The interpretation, application, and enforcement'.
perturbation.location: Page 2, after the sentence 'The interpretation, application, and enforcement of this Ag reement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Indiana without reference to choice of law principles.'
perturbation.contradicted_location: Page 2, after the sentence 'The interpretation, application, and enforcement of this Ag reement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Indiana without reference to choice of law principles.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: The interpretation, application, and enforcement of this Ag reement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Indiana without reference to choice of law principles. However, disputes concerning intellectual property rights shall be governed by Federal law. Any cl aim, suit, or cause of action involving the interpretation, application, or enforcement of this Agreement shall be commenced in Delaware County Circuit Court in Muncie, Indiana. Ball State University | Third-Party Non-Disclosure Agreement | Rev: 9/07/12 Page 3 of 4 r. This Agreement expresses the entire agreement and understa nding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements, commitment and unde rstandings pertaining to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction by stating that while the agreement is governed by Indiana law, intellectual property disputes are governed by Federal law. This creates uncertainty, because intellectual property issues might intersect with aspects of the agreement that are supposed to be governed by Indiana law. The original text establishes Indiana law as the governing law without exceptions. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, which means the contradiction exists within that paragraph.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement expresses the entire agreement and understa nding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements, commitment and unde rstandings pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any modifications of or changes to this Ag reement shall be in writing and signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement expresses the entire agreement and understa nding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements, commitment and unde rstandings pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any modifications of or changes to this Ag reement shall be valid if communicated via email and acknowledged by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement requires modifications to be 'in writing and signed by both parties.' The change allows modifications to be valid via email acknowledgment, contradicting the more formal requirement. This creates uncertainty about the legitimate process for altering the agreement, especially considering the need for tangible proof of agreement. This contradiction is occurring in the paragraph starting 'This Agreement expresses the entire agreement'.
perturbation.location: Page 3, after the sentence 'This Agreement expresses the entire agreement and understa nding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements, commitment and unde rstandings pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any modifications of or changes to this Ag reement shall be in writing and signed by both parties.'
perturbation.contradicted_location: Page 3, after the sentence 'This Agreement expresses the entire agreement and understa nding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements, commitment and unde rstandings pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any modifications of or changes to this Ag reement shall be in writing and signed by both parties.'
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement expresses the entire agreement and understa nding of the parties with respect to the subject matter hereof and supersedes all prior oral or written agreements, commitment and unde rstandings pertaining to the subject matter hereof. Any modifications of or changes to this Ag reement shall be valid if communicated via email and acknowledged by both parties. s. Unless earlier terminated in accordance with the provisions hereof, this Agreement shall remain in full force and effect for the duration of the Non-Disclosure Period, whereupon it shall expire. Either party may terminate this Agreement at any time, without cause, effective imme diately upon written notice of termin ation; however, in the event this Agreement is terminated prior to expiration of the Non-Disc losure Period, its provisions shall survive and remain in effect for the remainder of the Non-Disclosure Period, with respect to Confidential Information disclosed prior to the effective date of termination.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires modifications to be in writing and signed by both parties, which sets a formal requirement for changes. The changed text introduces an alternative method where modifications are valid if communicated via email and acknowledged, directly contradicting the original requirement for written and signed modifications. This contradiction creates ambiguity regarding the proper procedure for amending the agreement and undermines the need for documented evidence of consent. The explanation provided supports that this change causes contradiction in the paragraph starting 'This Agreement expresses the entire agreement'.
